subject.name: Construction
subject.description: Building fabric, drawings, materials and Building Regulations.

questions.text: On a 1:50 drawing, a wall measures 84 mm. What is its real length in metres?
Which scale is normally used for a location/site plan?
questions.explain: 1:1250 or 1:2500 for the location plan, 1:200/1:500 for the site plan, 1:50/1:100 for floor plans, 1:5/1:10 for details.

questions.text: Match each drawing abbreviation to its meaning.
questions.explain: These appear on nearly every set of construction drawings you'll ever open.

questions.text: A room is 5.4 m × 3.6 m. How many square metres of floor screed are needed, allowing 10% waste? (1 d.p.)

quizzes.title: Building Fabric & Regs
quizzes.description: Cavity walls, DPCs and the Approved Documents.

questions.text: What is the minimum height of a DPC above finished external ground level?
questions.explain: 150 mm (two brick courses) stops rain splash-back bridging the DPC.

questions.text: Match each Approved Document to its subject.

pairs.right: Access to and use of buildings

questions.explain: Part P is the one that brings domestic electrical work under Building Control.

questions.text: Wall ties in a cavity wall should slope slightly downwards towards the inner leaf.
questions.explain: They must slope down towards the OUTER leaf so any water runs away from the inside, and the drip must sit in the cavity.
